transcript.text: So where are you based in the U.S.?
Right now I'm in SF, but I actually live like regularly at the moment in Mexico, in a small town in Mexico.
Yes, yes.
But prior to that, for the last decade, I've been living in Long Beach, California, so in L.A.
Nice, nice.
So where you are now in Mexico, what's the weather like this season?
It's beautiful.
It's like chilly, but nice and sunny, and it's really good.
Please don't continue.
Where are you based again?
Can you remind me?
Berlin.
Berlin, that's right, that's right.
Berlin, yes.
So depressing at this time of year.
I have one of my best friends who lived in Berlin, and he was telling me, Berlin is incredible in the summer, but really rough in the winter.
Yeah, so true.
Hey, Isabel.
Hey, Isabel, how are you doing?
Hi.
Yeah, I'm good, thanks.
We were talking about how William is enjoying a nice weather in Mexico.
Nice.
Are you in Mexico City?
No, I'm about four hours north in a town called San Miguel de Allende.
It's a very pretty ancient town.
Cool.
Yeah, yeah.
Are you also based in Berlin, Isabelle?
Yeah.
And, yeah, no plans to go anywhere warm soon.
I feel like I've been in, like, three layers for the last three months.
Or where are you?
You're originally British, yes?
I'm half Irish, half Scottish, and I grew up in Belgium.
Oh, wow.
I don't have, like, an Irish or a Scottish accent.
Okay, that's really cool.
I went to, in neither of those places, but I went to uni in Sheffield, and I used to go up to...
Edinburgh all the time.
So my friend was going to school in Edinburgh.
So I know Scotland fairly well.
Really like it.
Good.
Yeah, I went to uni at Leeds, so not too far from Sheffield.
Wow.
I actually got into Leeds as well, and I was deciding between Sheffield and Leeds, and I made the mistake of choosing Sheffield.
Nice.
How come you went to the UK for uni?
So I'm half French, half American, and because of the French nationality at the time, could get local prices, which at the time was just £1,000 a year.
I think it was £1,160, and then they tripled it, and then they tripled it.

But I was very lucky at the time to be able to get a good education in the UK without having to get in huge debt, like my older sister did, who is, she's 46, and she's still paying off her student debt.
God, yeah.
But yeah, really, the good old days of UK.
Okay.
Like practically free education.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It was, it was the good old days.
Yep.
Well, I appreciate you guys.
Are we waiting for anybody else or are we good to go?
I think good to go.
Rachel might join, but I know she's been in quite a few meetings today.
Yeah.
No worries.
Let's go ahead and dig in.
So just FYI, I think you may know this, but I'm covering for Kathy for the next two weeks.
She is traveling in Hong Kong.
So, all right, let's go ahead and dig in.
Move this down here.
So just kicking things off, anything top of mind that you'd like to discuss?
I can't see you, so you gotta verbally say yes or no.
I have two questions, but I just wait for the end of the talk.
Maybe I get the answer during your presentation.
Yeah.
From my side, I just want to bring up the GEO data again and ask when we'll be able to get the benchmark of where we're sitting now.
Okay.
Let me actually just check real quickly if it's already set up.
There it is.
Hmm.
I know that she set this up.
So I think the thing is it was like still loading the information.
Okay.
Let me come back to you on this one.
I know that that's an important one.
Thank you.
Okay.
So GEO data can get access.
Cool.
Anything else?
All right.
So some quick updates.
We absorbed all that feedback that you guys shared.
And
And now you can see your content calendar here.
This is built up by a cluster.
So you can see each of the clusters here.
Wait, I'm in the wrong one.
I'm sorry.
Here it is.
Yeah, I was like, wait a minute, billing.
Yeah, no, no, sorry.
Wrong one.
Yeah, no, here they are.
So here are the clusters.
And then by those industry verticals that you guys had given us more direction on.
And then if you click on these little dropdowns, you can see all of them here.
And then in terms of like the status, you can see which ones are in production by, come on, load up.
In any case, you'll be able to, as we go through this process and go deeper into it, you'll be able to see which ones are in production, which ones are still being reviewed, et cetera.
So here, this one's in production.
These two are ready to start.
So they're already in the pipeline itself.
And I'm going to give you guys a little sneak peek of the pipeline because I think this really kind of builds more understanding and structure on what is actually happening.
But I think more than anything, it's helpful to look at it by cluster.

And really the hope that we have here is that you guys go through these and you can click on any one of these and then put a little comment if you have any comments on it or if you want to change it in some form or another.
But really just going through these, there's over 100 topics here with keywords that we think would work well within the clusters that we've now gotten approved from you guys.
This is great.
And I think, yeah, like you mentioned, very cool to see it come to life and have such a big pipeline of articles.
So in terms of what you mentioned, just going in and like adding comments.
So can we now assign priority?
Yes.
would you?
Okay.
Absolutely.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
And you can actually see right here, there's a priority tab.
So you can just go ahead and...
Click here and then give it the priority.
And so that will really help us be very clear about which ones you want to tackle first.
Nice.
I think that'd be great, Reza, if we could do that in the next week or so.
And maybe we just start with clarifying high and low.
Because I think maybe the ones around like composable commerce, I would say, are actually more like low prior.
Yeah, sure.
I'll do that.
Definitely.
Thanks.
By the next week, yeah.
Okay.
Super.
So that gives you a little sneak peek of that.
And now I wanted to actually walk you guys through, you know, the...
ahead.
Sorry.
One more question, actually.
Is there a view where we can see like an actual calendar view of when we can expect each post?
Is that something that you...
Well, typically not really for the sprint because when you're doing a calendar view and building a whole content calendar, you're talking probably like one to three months ahead of time.
But because we're just ramping up to five articles a week, it's really more so just about selecting which ones we're going to be working on during this phase one.
And then I think in phase two, then you build out a more full content calendar.
The thing is, we're still figuring out our system together with you, how long it takes for you guys to review it and then to get added to the CMS.
So hence why we don't try to stick too hard to any specific date just at this junction.
Not that we don't want to, to be clear.
It's definitely something that we'd like to be able to set up, but it's still something that's merging or coming together, if that makes sense.
Yeah, it sounds good.

Yeah, the reason I ask is because we have a kind of like wider all company content calendar where like the wider GTM team can also just check in and see what's coming up that I could potentially send.
Like a prospect or something like that.
So I agree.
It's something that would be great to have in the future.
Yeah.

And again, we can definitely work with you guys to put that together and make sure that you have that kind of, you know, not just visibility, but also, which is what you're describing, which is like your ability to plan and say, okay, I know that this is coming down the pipeline.
Let me go talk to so-and-so in product and so-and-so in PR and do a coordination.
So I'm with you.
Cool.
Okay.
So feel free to share any feedback in here.
And then I just want to give you guys, yeah, as I said, like a little peek under the hood of how all this works.
So first off, we worked with you guys on these core elements, which is our artifacts.
And here, in fact, there are some unique artifacts that we've built specifically for you guys, like this ecosystems partner one, for instance.
Um, and in each one of these, we fill in as many.
As information as we can, and then we update it as you give us this feedback.
So as we go through this, we're going to be sharing with you the calibration article by the end of this week.
You're going to share information on the calibration article, feedback, even themes that come up for you.
They're like, oh, you know, we actually really prefer this angle versus this angle.
Well, what we would do is we would go into the writing guidelines and update the bits of the writing guidelines, add specific elements, etc.
And so you can actually see, for instance, like there's a history section.
So you can always go back and look and see, okay, well, what was updated?
You know, how did we change?
So here you can see we've added some additional elements based off of either feedback or research or anything else that we're picking up from you guys.
All of this gets logged and added into our artifacts.
And then these artifacts get inserted dynamically into the actual AI pipelines.
So I'm going to show that to you.
Actually, before I do that, any questions on this part?
Okay, cool.

Also, you'll see here a couple of other additional core elements that you guys have shared with us, your case studies, internal statistics, and then we have a specific proof-free checklist that we've built out.
So now you can see we've built three agentic workflows.
The first is the just general article generation.
Then we've got comparisons, competitor articles, and then case studies.
So if I click into one of these, I'll see, you know, typically when we're really running fully, you'll have a long list of all the different articles that we're creating.
But in this case, we'll just use this as an example.
You can see each of the steps.
So you have to imagine that each one of these is a standalone agent.
So if I click on the research, inspect it, I can see all of the steps that are happening within this and how long it takes.
So this takes about a minute.
This is seven minutes, so on and so forth.
And each one of these is...
As I said, a kind of a standalone piece that will also grade its own results.
So to generate a research plan, it'll synthesize the results, it'll evaluate the research quality.
So you can see it's giving itself a score and it will continue reiterating on it until it gets to a high enough score, generate additional questions, so on and so forth.
So this is one small step within a sequence of steps that are happening that you can see here.
And each one of these then has that kind of agentic capability to continue to push and build to the best of their ability.
And you can see that in some cases, this is taking almost an hour of time as it's going through all of these steps and then iterating on it if it's not getting the right quality or structure.
So you can see that again here, planner, execute improvements, run comprehensive evaluation, planner, execute, run comprehensive.
right.
So it's doing this again and again and again, making sure that we are getting exactly the quality and.
also the sourcing that is reliable.
Okay.
Any questions around this?
I know this is, I kind of did a little fast path, the fast pass through of it, but I think it's always helpful to be able to share this and, yeah, just answer any questions or clarify anything.
Yeah, that sounds good.
And, but, so this is like where the articles sits in your platform before migrating them to a story block, right?
Yes, that's right.
This is the production layer.

So within the production layer, what we're doing is we're gathering all this great information that you guys have given us, the content strategy is locked in, and then we start the actual production layer.
So somebody is building this pipeline, it's a custom pipeline, based off of everything that you've given us, and then there's a managing editor that is going through and checking all the content.
There's three specific steps where the human goes in and makes sure that we're aligned, it's structured in the right way.
And at the very end, they do a full editorial pass on it.
That is what the results that get passed on to you.
And then your feedback then gets ingested.
Some of your feedback is just going to be very like, it's just for that piece, for that instance, and we just got to make that little change.
Some of that feedback will be more general, sort of what you could call it meta feedback.
Where you're saying, hey, actually, as a whole, this is something that I'm seeing that I want to change.
And then we would go back into those artifacts that I just shared with you, and we would make changes within that.
And if it's something larger or bigger, then we would actually bring in our engineering team who would go and tweak the pipeline itself.
So some of the core prompting and structuring and the code behind it to make it get the results that we're looking for.
Thanks.
Hi, Rachel.
We're just running.
through the updates from last week, but all looking very cool.

And William, I was also wondering, what does the process look like for interlinking with all the new articles that you're creating, but then also going back as we get more and more new articles to make sure that all the content, all the topic clusters are basically interlinked?
Yeah, that's a really good question.

So there's two answers to that, which is one, there's an automated process that's constantly looking at the sitemap and looking at the, like there's literally an agentic step, which is specifically for internal linking.
And it's looking at, you know, what is relevant to this piece into this specific section, because it'll chunk the content into smaller chunks and that'll see what can interlink.
So that's an automatic layer.
There is the second part to your question, which is, well, we may want to go back to some piece.
That we started with, and with the 30 or odd pieces that we've now linked, we want to go back and link those up.
That would be more of a manual process.
And so for that, we would go to our managing editor and say, hey, this chunk of articles we think is really relevant to this.
Please go back and find specific places where we can integrate that.
Sounds good.
And that's managed by your team?
Mm-hmm.
Yes.
Yeah.
And then I was also wondering, because we have so many old blog posts, which I think some of the keywords have crossover with some of the keywords from the content calendar.
In that case, would you go back and revamp those blog posts, or would you recommend that we unpublish those and create entirely new ones?
It's very case by case.
I would say the general rule of thumb is optimize or update if you can.
Because it's almost always better to do that.
There are some cases where, let's say, the URL structure, like the slug, is not optimized.
It's not very good.
In which case, you most likely just sunset that article and then redirect the link to the new piece.
That is a lot more work and not always necessarily better.
You would only really do that if the slug or the structure is really not good.
It's really not optimized for it, but it's still ancillary enough that it creates an issue.
So, obviously, I saw that this is like a core element for Challenge 1 is you guys have a lot of content and we want to optimize it.
Your first calibration article is an optimization.
So, I think that definitely is a really good play and will get you results faster.
That sounds good.
And, again, that would kind of be suggested by the GrowthX team.
So, it would be kind of part of the process that you manage.
Yeah, exactly.
We're always going to do a combination of net new content, of course, like that.
I think that's a good signal for Google is to see us not just optimizing or refreshing content, but also creating a lot of good, high-quality, new content.
So we'll do a mixture of the two, but I can definitely see how optimizations would be a good path forward for us.
Sounds good.
Yeah.
Great.
Rachel, anything on your mind?
At the beginning of the meeting, we asked anything top of mind.
So I just want to ask you real quickly.
Yeah, I'm sure the team, sorry for being, I really wanted to join, but something came up, I had to.
No worries.
I'm sure we did, we did talk about the Gentic fully, Isabelle, all that and bringing that in or reza, sorry.
That's, that's my question for.
Okay, go for it.
I just waited for all the platforms.
Yeah.
But yeah, so I basically had two questions.
One of them was asked by Isabelle about GeoData that you said it's like in the works.
And yeah, the other question is about a...

It's a big topic for the company at the moment, so we are working on it, and over the past few weeks, we've produced four blog posts, and we have one report, which is a work in progress, aiming to publish it mid-February.
So our question is, is it advisable from your side that we continue to produce content on the topic, or is it a good spot to pause a little bit or slow down a little bit and then create new content?
So how do you see, like, basically, how do you see that?

Is it, like, because it's, like, it's a new topic for the landscape of loyalty and promotions, and we have produced, like, a protocol, which is absolutely new, and it seems that we are now in a better place comparing us with our competitors.
We definitely are in a better place.
But so should we continue producing at a speed like this or perhaps slow down a little bit?
What do you think about it?
So just to clarify, I want to understand what your concern would be.

If you were to produce this content on your end and we are building these clusters that you guys are overseeing and making sure that there's no sort of crossover, what is your concern that having these two streams happening at the same time?
Just want to understand that.
So, no, no, it's not like a big concern.
It's like we just want to make it clear from like in terms of the workload from our side.
So if like if this is like in your plan to produce it, so maybe we can like slow down a little bit on our side.
If like or if it like doesn't hurt, we produce content and then you have it on your calendars.
So just basically syncing.
We want to like to be synced with you guys.
Yeah, I mean, I think.
Yeah.
In general, I feel it's also like.
Yeah.
How many pieces do we need to basically become like a category leader when it comes to agentic commerce?
And yeah, what advice you have for us to keep on building that category authority?
Okay, got it.
I think I'm following now.
So you're building this new content and you really want to own this, conversation around agentic commerce.
Like that is the goal.
And the question is, should you guys be building this stuff or should you kind of be handing it to us?
Or should we be doing a combination of the two where you're creating content?
We're also creating content.
We're coordinating efforts, ultimately with the goal to own conversation on agentic commerce.
Yeah.
And I think like how much time do we invest in it?
Because like the last two weeks, we've been super focused on this.
But of course, we have like other campaigns and other things that we need to be focusing on.
Let me, I want to do a quick gut check.
Well,
Perhaps this is also something that you can share, reza, is what is the, if this is such a new thing, how much search volume is there that you've seen?
Are you using SEMrush or anything like that?
Yeah, I did.
Yeah.
So I'll share it with you after the call, but I think it isn't really that much.
So yeah.
Okay.
That's what I assumed.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It's not really that much.
And especially when you use loyalty and promotions as keywords, it's absolutely low.
But yeah.
But I think, yeah.
I think the only competitor near us in terms of keywords volume is Voucherify and we are leading.
Definitely we are leading there.
Okay.
It does look pretty competitive.
It's got a keyword difficulty of 70.
So there's, okay.
OpenAI is the number one.
And then we've got Salesforce, McKinsey, Stripe.
It's pretty tough.
So I think to answer your question, it's not a yes or no, to be honest.
This is a complex one because the advantage of you guys doing this is that you have deep knowledge.

And so typically what works the best is if there's an internal team already doing content, that that team is saying, okay, well, we have this deep knowledge, this internal expertise, this proprietary data.
We're going to create these, like you were saying, you're building a report, right?
A report, for instance, like a deep report, that's something that would be much harder for GrowthX to do.
We could do it, but it would take a lot more time.
We'd have to build a whole authentic flow for this.
While in your case, you could just work internally with your product manager, whoever it is that you're working with to build this stuff.
And that gives you deep, deep authority because you're bringing this unique perspective and creating a very in-depth piece for this.
Now,
Now, should that be the only thing you do?

No, you should also, if this is a very competitive space, which it appears to be, is you want to, the same strategy we're talking about with clusters, you want to create sort of a bubble, a cluster of content around this topic.

Now, because it's such a brand new topic, and there isn't actually that much organic search for it, because it's just not that many people know agentic commerce, then that's both an opportunity and a challenge, because it's an opportunity because there's probably not a ton of players in this space.
Although, again, doing that quick look, it looks like there is already some pretty big players.
But then the other, the disadvantage is that, yeah, you're now really competing in this space, which just doesn't have a lot of search volume, that's not naturally being searched a lot.
I think if we want to really go after this, we would want to create a new cluster that's specific to this.
I would say do not stop your efforts.
You should still create this content that you're creating.
And in this case, it will be a very tight-in strategy with us and what you guys are doing.
So if you have these four blog posts and a report that's coming out, what content can we do that is ancillary or parallel to those pieces?
And that will be very easy for us to interlink because then again, it gives more power to those pieces.

And then if you have anything else that you can do from a backlinking perspective or any partners that you can have that can link back to your content and give it a bit of a boost, I think that will really help, you know, own that space.
But it's obviously competitive and so it's going to be a lot of work.
Just one, I think, point on that is that we are not, we're not looking to compete with Salesforce or Anthropic or whomever, you know, was obviously we're not going to win.
Because the reality is we're a part of their ecosystem.
So it's like maybe we need to get more creative about it's not owning agentic commerce.

What we want people to know about us is if you are searching agentic commerce and you're looking at solutions like a Salesforce or one of the bigger companies and you are trying to figure out how to do better commerce marketing, thinking about the shopper experience, and loyalty is a part of that.
We want to show up for like agentic plus loyalty, agentic plus promotion, agentic plus tier tier program, like agentic plus like price devaluation.
don't like there's going to be it's that next layer under agentic commerce.
But we're we're we're not as ambitious in this that we need to be at that level.
We want to like after you found that, you know, that world, you're thinking of optimizing it.
Like we're one of the first players that comes up in those I don't changes.

I don't know if niche is the right word here, but if you want to be a category leader of that niche or of that category, when it comes to agent e-commerce in loyalty and promotion, definitely we can't compete with open AI.
But when it comes to loyalty, promotions, and agent in commons, or AI, I don't know, AI-driven incentives, like this kind of keywords, we definitely want to be that category leader.
Okay.
What I would love to do here is, so the quick answer is we should do this.
Like, I think growthx can absolutely help you with this.
I think it's going to take a little bit more coordination between what you guys are building and building it.
Yeah, it's like a slightly different cluster.

It's a combined cluster with what you guys are building and how do we interlink and how do we make sure that we're structuring what we're going to be producing in a way that supports that goal and specifically the niche that Rachel's talking about.
So...

So I think next steps here is if you want to share the topics and then share any other details you have about specific niche or long tail of, you know, for instance, identic commerce for loyalty programs or something like that, and we can start to get a little bit clearer on what that is, I think it's worth, it's absolutely worth combining our efforts.
That's great.
I can do that.
I can take that over.
Thanks, Reza.

I was thinking also it could be worth, because we're doing verticalization with, like, the upcoming growthx content strategy, so we could do agentic commerce for retail, for QSR, for maybe less for financial services, because I don't really see how that would work.
But I think that would be, like, an easy way to plug it in with what we're already doing.
Okay, perfect.
One question on that, like, maybe devil's advocate, if you have any expertise of, like...
And even that, like, should we be careful about even agentic commerce for X vertical when we're really looking to be within, there's going to be so many dimensions.
It's like agentic commerce is like the new mobile, right?
And so it's like, we're going to own mobile.
We want to own, you know, the loyalty aspect of like, you know, agentic.

Like, and so when it comes to how you build programs, and so is there anything like we should be thinking about differently to support that objective than maybe like our, the way that we're coming about it that you've just seen through, I know you work with so many brands that, you know, have very niche spaces and, and is there, yeah, just curious if, and maybe the answer's no, it's fine to keep what you're doing.
But I just wanted to mention that is that we're, we don't know what we don't around it.
So, I mean, what that really what that brings up.
For me, it's like we just need to do a lot more research with SEMrush and Google Search Console and those kinds of things to see what people are searching.
Because I think that's really the answer to that question, which is, to your point, you don't want to own mobile, but you want to own this very specific thing.
But we don't know, just off the top of our heads, how are people specifically searching for this?
Is anybody typing in a Gentic loyalty program, for instance?
Would that be an option?
I did a quick search just now, I don't see any search volume for that, for instance.
But it's a little bit of a, because it's also brand new, it's a little bit of an investigation that we have to do.
So that's something I think, Riza, you and I can both be doing on our own ends.
I definitely need a little bit more direction from you guys so that I can start to go deep into that.

But I think that's the path that we need to take here, is that we're not trying to own mobile, we're not trying to own Gentic Commerce, but we want to own something within that, let's get very specific about what that is.
And see, okay, what is it?
Are people really searching for this?
And then finally, how competitive is this?
And depending on how competitive it is, then we can decide, okay, how do we build a plan so that we can really win here?
Great.
Cool.
I got to jump.
have another call.
But thank you so much, everybody.
And we'll be in touch.
Thanks.
Bye.
Thanks.
Bye.
The recording has stopped.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review the new content calendar and align on the "agentic commerce" strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [The new content calendar is live, organized by cluster and vertical. The team will prioritize topics by next week to guide production.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [A new "agentic commerce" strategy was defined: target the niche of "agentic commerce for loyalty/promotions" instead of the broad, highly competitive "agentic commerce" space.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1541.0)
  - [GrowthX will research search intent for this niche. The internal team will continue creating deep-expertise content (e.g., reports), which GrowthX will support with ancillary articles.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1393.0)
  - [GrowthX will provide the GEO data benchmark report and the first calibration article (an optimization piece) by the end of the week.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)

## Topics

### Content Calendar & Production Process

  - [The new content calendar is live, organized by cluster and industry vertical.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [**Action:** The team will use the calendar's priority tab to mark topics as "high" or "low" priority within the next week.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [**Production Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=575.0)
      - [GrowthX uses custom "agentic workflows" that dynamically pull from client artifacts (writing guidelines, case studies) to generate content.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=575.0)
      - [Agents self-evaluate and iterate to ensure quality before a human editor performs a final editorial pass.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
      - [Feedback updates the artifacts, improving future content.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=854.0)
  - [**Interlinking Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=906.0)
      - [New articles are automatically interlinked by an agent that scans the sitemap.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=906.0)
      - [GrowthX's managing editor will manually interlink older articles to new, relevant content.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=953.0)
  - [**Content Optimization:**](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=988.0)
      - [The default is to optimize existing articles, as this typically yields faster results.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=988.0)
      - [New articles are created only if an old article's URL slug is unoptimized, requiring a redirect.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1001.0)

### "Agentic Commerce" Content Strategy

  - [**Problem:** The team questioned whether to continue producing content on "agentic commerce," given its newness and low search volume.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1138.0)
  - [**Initial Analysis:** The broad "agentic commerce" topic is highly competitive (SEMrush KD 70), dominated by major players like OpenAI and Salesforce.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)
  - [**Strategic Pivot:** The goal is not to compete with these giants but to own a specific niche.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1541.0)
      - [**Target Niche:** "Agentic commerce for loyalty and promotions."](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This positions the company as a key solution for businesses already exploring agentic commerce and looking to optimize their loyalty programs.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
  - [**Execution Plan:**](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1393.0)
      - [**Internal Team:** Continue creating deep-expertise content (e.g., the upcoming report) that GrowthX cannot easily replicate.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1393.0)
      - [**GrowthX:** Create ancillary articles that support the internal team's content, forming a powerful, interlinked cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1500.0)
      - [**Research:** GrowthX and Reza will investigate search intent for this niche to validate the strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1797.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)
      - [Provide the GEO data benchmark report.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)
      - [Deliver the first calibration article (an optimization piece).](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1049.0)
  - [**Reza:**](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1680.0)
      - [Share topics and details for the "agentic commerce for loyalty/promotions" niche.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1680.0)
      - [Research search intent for the new niche.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=1797.0)
  - [**Isabelle & Reza:**](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
      - [Prioritize topics in the content calendar by next week.](https://fathom.video/share/KtgCLyFyGKEVhRK_SQ8HhEXb9EFeYjsy?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)

action_items.description: Confirm GEO data access/status; update Isabelle
Email William SEMrush/GSC data + agentic commerce topics/details
